title: AWS Lambda MicroVM Sandboxed Code Execution
description: Deploy a Lambda MicroVM that executes arbitrary Python code in an isolated Firecracker VM with sub-second startup via snapshots.

introBox.headline: How it works
text: This pattern deploys a Lambda MicroVM running a sandboxed code execution service. Clients submit Python code via HTTP POST, and the MicroVM executes it in an isolated subprocess, returning stdout, stderr, and exit code.

The MicroVM image is built from a Dockerfile that Lambda executes server-side. During the build, the application starts and signals readiness via the /ready lifecycle hook. Lambda then takes a memory and disk snapshot, enabling sub-second cold starts for all future launches.

The MicroVM supports suspend/resume — it automatically suspends after idle timeout and resumes in under a second when new traffic arrives. Each MicroVM runs in its own Firecracker VM with hardware-level isolation.

bullets.text: Firecracker - Secure and fast microVMs for serverless computing

text: See the GitHub repo for detailed testing instructions.

authors.name: Alexander Vladimirov
authors.bio: Senior Serverless Solutions Architect at AWS, specializing in Agentic AI Workloads.
